records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disabled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disabling (?).]

defs: To render unable or incapable; to destroy the force, vigor, or power of action of; to deprive of competent physical or intellectual power; to incapacitate; to disqualify; to make incompetent or unfit for service; to impair.
quotes: A Christian's life is a perpetual exercise, a wrestling and warfare, for which sensual pleasure disables him.
And had performed it, if my known offense Had not disabled me.

defs: To deprive of legal right or qualification; to render legally incapable.
quotes: An attainder of the ancestor corrupts the blood, and disables his children to inherit.

defs: To deprive of that which gives value or estimation; to declare lacking in competency; to disparage; to undervalue.
